enrichment.short_description: Open-source subscription billing and payments platform you self-host for full data control.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kill Bill is a free, open-source (Apache 2.0) billing platform for subscription, usage-based, and multi-tenant billing you self-host.

features.title: Subscription billing engine
features.description: Supports usage-based, prepaid, postpaid, and multi-tenant subscription models.

features.title: Payment gateway plugins
features.description: Native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.

features.title: Kaui admin UI
features.description: A back-office web interface for managing subscriptions and payments.

features.title: Plugin architecture
features.description: An extensible framework for building custom integrations and modifications.

features.title: Tax engine integrations
features.description: Connects to tax and compliance engines such as Avalara and Vertex.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Deploys via Docker or Kubernetes in your own infrastructure, avoiding SaaS lock-in.

pros: Core platform is free and fully open source under Apache 2.0
Full data ownership since it runs in your own infrastructure
No per-transaction or revenue-percentage fees
Long track record, in production for over 15 years
Plugin architecture supports many payment gateways and tax engines
cons: Requires engineering resources to deploy, operate, and maintain
No official low-code hosted option from the core project itself (managed hosting is via third parties)
Enterprise accelerator (Aviate) is a separate proprietary offering, not part of the free core

faqs.question: Is Kill Bill free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Kill Bill platform is open source under the Apache License 2.0 and free to self-host.

faqs.question: Do I need to host Kill Bill myself?
faqs.answer: Kill Bill is designed to be self-hosted via Docker or Kubernetes; third-party providers such as ChaChing offer managed hosting.

faqs.question: What payment gateways does Kill Bill support?
faqs.answer: It has native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.

faqs.question: What language is Kill Bill written in?
faqs.answer: The codebase is primarily Java, with smaller portions in JavaScript, CSS, Shell, and PL/pgSQL.

use_cases.title: Custom subscription billing
use_cases.description: Engineering teams that need full control over billing logic build on Kill Bill instead of a black-box SaaS billing vendor.
use_cases.audience: Engineering and finance teams at growth-stage startups

use_cases.title: Regulated or high-volume billing
use_cases.description: Enterprises with compliance or data-residency requirements self-host Kill Bill to keep billing data in-house.
use_cases.audience: Enterprises with compliance requirements

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kill Bill?
paragraphs: Kill Bill is an open-source billing and payments platform, released under the Apache 2.0 license, that handles subscription billing, usage-based billing, and payment processing.
It was founded independently in 2010 and is designed to be self-hosted, giving teams full control over their billing data and logic instead of relying on a third-party SaaS billing vendor.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kill Bill is for
paragraphs: Kill Bill suits engineering teams that want to build custom billing logic without vendor lock-in or per-transaction fees, and organizations with compliance or data-residency needs that require self-hosted billing infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Revenue management platform that automates quote-to-cash billing, invoicing, and usage metering for SaaS companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hyperline automates SaaS billing, CPQ, invoicing, and usage-based metering, starting at $199/month plus a percentage of revenue.

features.title: CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote)
features.description: Streamlines quote creation and contract management for sales teams.

features.title: Automated billing and invoicing
features.description: Handles flexible pricing models, invoicing automation, and revenue recognition.

features.title: Usage-based billing
features.description: Real-time event ingestion, metering, and consumption-based pricing rules.

features.title: Global invoicing compliance
features.description: Supports compliant invoicing and e-invoicing certification across 100+ countries.

features.title: Native payment integrations
features.description: Connects to Stripe, GoCardless, Mollie, and Airwallex for payment collection.

features.title: CRM and accounting integrations
features.description: Integrates with tools such as HubSpot, Salesforce, and Attio (add-on pricing applies).

pros: Combines CPQ, billing, and usage metering in one platform
Global invoicing compliance across 100+ countries
Offers a free trial with 10 invoices and no credit card required
SOC2, ISO27001, and GDPR certified
cons: Pricing includes a percentage-of-revenue fee on top of the monthly base price
CRM and accounting integrations cost extra per integration
High-volume tier requires contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: How much does Hyperline cost?
faqs.answer: The Quote to Cash plan starts at $199/month plus 0.6% of revenue; a usage-billing tier is $299/month plus 0.7% of revenue, and a high-volume enterprise tier is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Does Hyperline offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Quote to Cash plan includes 10 free invoices to start, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What payment providers does Hyperline integrate with?
faqs.answer: It natively integrates with Stripe, GoCardless, Mollie, and Airwallex.

faqs.question: Where is Hyperline based?
faqs.answer: Hyperline is headquartered in Paris, France.

use_cases.title: SaaS quote-to-cash automation
use_cases.description: Sales and finance teams use Hyperline to move from quote creation to invoicing and revenue recognition in one workflow.
use_cases.audience: Finance and revenue operations teams at SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Usage-based billing
use_cases.description: Companies with metered or seat-based pricing use Hyperline to track consumption in real time and bill accurately.
use_cases.audience: Engineering and finance teams billing on usage

features: Native Stripe, GoCardless, Mollie, Airwallex integrations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hyperline?
paragraphs: Hyperline is a revenue management platform, based in Paris, that automates the quote-to-cash process for subscription businesses, combining CPQ, billing, invoicing, and usage-based metering.
It connects to CRMs and payment providers to automate the flow from a sales quote through to compliant global invoicing and revenue recognition.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hyperline is for
paragraphs: Hyperline is built for finance and revenue operations teams at SaaS and subscription companies that need to automate billing across complex pricing models, including usage-based and seat-based plans.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source billing platform that sits between Stripe and your app to handle plans, usage tracking, and AI credits.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Autumn is an open-source (Apache 2.0) billing layer over Stripe for usage tracking, feature gating, and AI credit billing.

features.title: Unified billing API
features.description: One API for managing plans, usage tracking, and AI credit systems.

features.title: Stripe integration layer
features.description: Sits on top of Stripe to handle upgrades, downgrades, cancellations, and failed payments without manual webhook handling.

features.title: Feature gating (check)
features.description: Verifies whether a customer has access to a given feature before granting it.

features.title: Usage tracking (track)
features.description: Records usage-based events for metered billing.

features.title: Self-serve and enterprise billing
features.description: Supports both self-serve payment flows and custom enterprise deals from one source of truth.

features.title: Open-source self-hosting
features.description: The full platform is open source (Apache 2.0) on GitHub and can be self-hosted.

pros: Fully open source under Apache 2.0, with source available on GitHub
Removes the need to build custom usage-tracking and billing logic
Purpose-built for AI startups billing on usage and AI credits
Used in production by companies including Mintlify and Firecrawl
cons: Detailed cloud pricing tiers are not fully published
Best suited to teams already built around Stripe
Newer project with a smaller ecosystem than established billing platforms

faqs.question: Is Autumn open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Autumn is open source under the Apache 2.0 license, with its source code on GitHub.

faqs.question: Does Autumn replace Stripe?
faqs.answer: No, Autumn sits on top of Stripe as a billing logic layer, handling plans, usage, and upgrades/downgrades rather than replacing payment processing.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Autumn?
faqs.answer: Yes, since the project is open source, though the quickest way to start is through Autumn's hosted cloud service.

faqs.question: What is Autumn used for?
faqs.answer: It is used to manage subscription plans, usage-based billing, feature gating, and AI credit systems, particularly for AI startups.

use_cases.title: AI credit billing
use_cases.description: AI startups use Autumn to meter and bill for AI usage credits without building custom billing infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: AI and SaaS startups

use_cases.title: Feature gating and usage tracking
use_cases.description: Engineering teams use Autumn's check and track APIs to gate features and record usage events tied to Stripe subscriptions.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams building on Stripe

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Autumn?
paragraphs: Autumn is an open-source pricing and billing platform, licensed under Apache 2.0, that acts as a layer between Stripe and an application to manage plans, usage tracking, and AI credit systems.
It provides API functions for attaching purchase flows, checking feature access, and tracking usage events, removing the need to hand-build billing logic around Stripe webhooks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Autumn is for
paragraphs: Autumn is aimed at AI and SaaS startups that need flexible, usage-based billing, including AI credit metering, and want a source of truth spanning both self-serve payments and enterprise deals.

enrichment.short_description: Free subscription analytics tool from Paddle that tracks MRR, churn, and other SaaS metrics.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ProfitWell Metrics by Paddle is a free subscription analytics tool tracking MRR, churn, LTV, and benchmarking against 30,000+ companies.

features.title: Revenue reporting
features.description: Tracks core subscription KPIs including MRR, ARR, churn, and customer lifetime value.

features.title: Customer health scoring
features.description: Identifies customers likely to convert, upgrade, or churn.

features.title: Industry benchmarking
features.description: Compares performance against a dataset of over 30,000 subscription companies.

features.title: Segmentation
features.description: Analyzes customer cohorts and pricing-plan data to surface growth trends.

features.title: Billing system integrations
features.description: Connects to Paddle, Stripe, Chargebee, Braintree, Zuora, and Recurly.

pros: Core analytics dashboard is completely free
Works with multiple billing systems, not just Paddle
Benchmarks against a large dataset of subscription companies
Backed by Paddle, an established payments company
cons: Now owned and operated by Paddle rather than as an independent standalone company
Advanced retention/pricing tools once bundled under the ProfitWell brand have been folded into Paddle's broader product line

faqs.question: Is ProfitWell free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ProfitWell Metrics is completely free to use.

faqs.question: Is ProfitWell still an independent company?
faqs.answer: No, ProfitWell was acquired and is now operated as ProfitWell Metrics, part of Paddle's product ecosystem.

faqs.question: What billing systems does ProfitWell Metrics work with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Paddle, Stripe, Chargebee, Braintree, Zuora, and Recurly, among others.

use_cases.title: SaaS metrics tracking
use_cases.description: Finance and founder teams use ProfitWell Metrics to monitor MRR, churn, and LTV without building custom reporting.
use_cases.audience: SaaS founders and finance teams

use_cases.title: Competitive benchmarking
use_cases.description: Companies compare their subscription metrics against a large dataset of peer companies.
use_cases.audience: SaaS operators evaluating growth performance

features: MRR, ARR, churn, and LTV dashboards
Segmentation by cohort and pricing plan

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ProfitWell?
paragraphs: ProfitWell (now branded ProfitWell Metrics) is a free subscription analytics tool that reports on MRR, ARR, churn, and customer lifetime value for SaaS businesses.
It was acquired and is now part of Paddle, and it continues to operate as a free tool that works across multiple billing systems, not only Paddle's own.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ProfitWell is for
paragraphs: ProfitWell suits SaaS founders and finance teams who want a free, ready-made view of subscription revenue metrics and benchmarking against other subscription companies.

enrichment.short_description: Customer success platform combining data, health scoring, and AI-driven automation for CSM teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vitally is an AI-powered customer success platform with health scoring, playbooks, and automation for B2B SaaS teams.

features.title: Vitally AI
features.description: Generates customer summaries, follow-up tasks, and insights from conversations and unstructured data.

features.title: Health scores
features.description: Dynamic customer health assessment based on lifecycle stage or segment.

features.title: Automation and playbooks
features.description: Automates CSM assignment, task creation, and other customer management workflows.

features.title: Project management
features.description: Agile-style project tracking to guide customers through onboarding milestones.

features.title: Customer surveys and NPS
features.description: Built-in feedback collection tools for measuring customer sentiment.

features.title: Reporting and dashboards
features.description: KPI visualization and team performance analytics.

pros: AI features (Vitally AI) built directly into the workflow for summaries and task generation
Includes unlimited automations and unlimited observer seats on all plans
High G2 rating (4.5 stars from 700+ reviews)
Serves 600+ B2B SaaS organizations including Zapier and Pleo
cons: Pricing is not published; requires contacting sales for a quote
Three plan tiers (Tech-Touch, Hybrid-Touch, High-Touch) require evaluation to determine fit

faqs.question: How much does Vitally cost?
faqs.answer: Vitally does not publish pricing; you need to request a quote for its Tech-Touch, Hybrid-Touch, or High-Touch plans.

faqs.question: Does Vitally include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vitally AI generates customer summaries, follow-up tasks, and insights from customer data.

faqs.question: Who uses Vitally?
faqs.answer: Vitally serves B2B SaaS companies including Zapier, Gorgias, Navattic, Pleo, and Productboard.

use_cases.title: Customer health monitoring
use_cases.description: CS teams track dynamic health scores to identify at-risk or expansion-ready accounts.
use_cases.audience: Customer success managers and CS leaders

use_cases.title: CS workflow automation
use_cases.description: Teams use playbooks and automation to standardize onboarding and renewal processes.
use_cases.audience: CS operations teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vitally?
paragraphs: Vitally is a customer success platform that combines customer data, health scoring, project management, and AI-driven automation in one workspace for CSM teams.
Its Vitally AI feature generates customer summaries, follow-up tasks, and insights drawn from conversations and unstructured customer data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vitally is for
paragraphs: Vitally is built for customer success managers, CS leaders, and CS operations teams at B2B SaaS companies looking to scale account management with automation and health scoring.

enrichment.short_description: Agentic customer platform combining CRM, customer success, and professional services automation for B2B teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Planhat is an agentic customer platform unifying CRM, customer success, and professional services automation for B2B teams.

features.title: Agentic automation
features.description: Delegates customer lifecycle processes to governed agents for faster execution.

features.title: Unified customer model
features.description: Builds a comprehensive customer profile integrating CRM data, time-series data, and external telemetry.

features.title: Multi-application platform
features.description: Functions as a Customer Success Platform, CRM, or Professional Services Automation tool.

features.title: AI-enhanced health scoring
features.description: Dynamically scores customer health and automatically captures touchpoints.

features.title: CRM integrations
features.description: Syncs with major CRM systems to keep customer data unified.

features.title: MCP server for Claude
features.description: Offers an MCP server integration for use with Anthropic's Claude.

pros: Combines CRM, customer success, and PSA in a single platform
Recognized on G2's Momentum Grid and Gartner's MarketScape/Magic Quadrant for CSM platforms
Serves companies across SaaS, IT services, healthcare, and security verticals
Offers forward-deployed implementation and consulting support
cons: No public pricing; requires an enquiry/quote for every plan
Add-ons like the upgraded AI platform and advanced portals are priced separately
Breadth of the platform (CRM + CSP + PSA) can mean a longer evaluation process

faqs.question: How much does Planhat cost?
faqs.answer: Planhat uses a custom, quote-based pricing model; you must contact sales via an 'Enquire' request for pricing.

faqs.question: Is Planhat just a customer success platform?
faqs.answer: No, Planhat can function as a Customer Success Platform, a CRM, or Professional Services Automation software, or a combination of all three.

faqs.question: Does Planhat integrate with AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an MCP Server integration with Anthropic's Claude and includes AI-enhanced health scoring.

use_cases.title: Unified customer lifecycle management
use_cases.description: Commercial teams use Planhat to manage sales, onboarding, and renewals from one unified customer model.
use_cases.audience: B2B commercial and customer success teams

use_cases.title: Professional services delivery
use_cases.description: Teams delivering implementation and services use Planhat's PSA capabilities alongside CS workflows.
use_cases.audience: Professional services and implementation teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Planhat?
paragraphs: Planhat is an agentic customer platform for B2B commercial teams that unifies CRM, customer success, and professional services automation around a single customer data model.
It uses governed AI agents to automate processes across the customer lifecycle and offers integrations including CRM syncing and an MCP server for Anthropic's Claude.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Planhat is for
paragraphs: Planhat targets B2B enterprises and commercial teams, including sales, customer success, and professional services, across startups, mid-market, and enterprise companies.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native customer success platform for SaaS teams to score customer health, detect churn risk, and automate playbooks.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Custify is an AI-native customer success platform with churn detection, health scoring, and automated playbooks for SaaS teams.

features.title: Customer 360
features.description: A unified view of customer adoption and usage patterns.

features.title: Customer health scoring
features.description: KPI-based health assessment across a customer portfolio.

features.title: Churn detection
features.description: AI-powered early warning system that flags at-risk accounts.

features.title: Expansion engine
features.description: Identifies upsell and renewal opportunities among existing customers.

features.title: Automation and playbooks
features.description: Converts business processes into automated workflows and task routing.

features.title: CustifyAI
features.description: Generates playbooks, account summaries, and sentiment analysis.

pros: Native integrations with 200+ tools including HubSpot, Salesforce, and Zendesk, plus Zapier
SOC 2 certified
Concierge onboarding with implementation completed within 4 weeks
AI-driven churn detection and playbook generation built in
cons: Pricing is not published; visitors must contact the pricing page or sales for details
Aimed primarily at subscription-based SaaS companies, less suited to non-SaaS businesses

faqs.question: How much does Custify cost?
faqs.answer: Custify does not publish specific pricing tiers on its site; interested customers need to check the pricing page or contact sales.

faqs.question: What integrations does Custify support?
faqs.answer: Custify connects with over 200 tools, including HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, Intercom, Zendesk, and Pipedrive via native integrations and Zapier.

faqs.question: Is Custify SOC 2 certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, Custify holds SOC 2 certification.

use_cases.title: Churn prevention
use_cases.description: CS teams use Custify's AI-powered health scoring and churn detection to identify at-risk accounts early.
use_cases.audience: Customer success managers at SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Expansion revenue
use_cases.description: Teams use the expansion engine to surface upsell and renewal opportunities across their customer base.
use_cases.audience: Customer success and account management teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Custify?
paragraphs: Custify is an AI-native customer success platform that centralizes customer data, automates workflows, and helps SaaS teams reduce churn and identify growth opportunities.
It includes CustifyAI for generating playbooks, summaries, and sentiment analysis, alongside customer health scoring and a churn-detection early warning system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Custify is for
paragraphs: Custify is built for subscription-based SaaS companies and their customer success managers who want to scale account management without proportionally growing headcount.

enrichment.short_description: Customer success platform with health scoring, journey management, and an AI co-pilot to reduce churn.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ClientSuccess offers SuccessScore health tracking, journey management, and an AI co-pilot to help B2B SaaS teams reduce churn.

features.title: SuccessScore
features.description: A 360-degree customer health tracking system combining usage, adoption, feedback, and sentiment data.

features.title: Pulse
features.description: Weekly and real-time reporting on account status.

features.title: Customer journey management
features.description: Manages the customer lifecycle including onboarding, adoption, and renewal.

features.title: SmartCS
features.description: An AI co-pilot that provides automated communications and generative AI features.

features.title: Customer portals
features.description: Branded portals with automated project templates for collaborative customer engagement.

features.title: NPS Analyzer
features.description: Tracks and analyzes Net Promoter Score data.

pros: Pricing scales by number of CSM users and modules needed, avoiding a one-size-fits-all plan
No setup or implementation fees
Offers 15-20% discounts for annual contracts
AICPA SOC certified
Recently acquired Product Signals to add product feedback capabilities
cons: Specific dollar pricing is not published; requires a custom quote
Startup package caps at 10 customers and 500 full licenses, requiring an upgrade as accounts grow

faqs.question: How is ClientSuccess priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is based on the number of customer success managers using the platform and the modules selected, across Startup, Growth, and Enterprise packages, with no public list price.

faqs.question: Does ClientSuccess offer an AI feature?
faqs.answer: Yes, SmartCS is an AI co-pilot that provides automated communications and generative AI capabilities.

faqs.question: Are there discounts for annual billing?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClientSuccess offers roughly 15-20% off month-to-month rates for annual contracts, plus multi-year discounts.

use_cases.title: Churn reduction
use_cases.description: CS teams use SuccessScore and Pulse reporting to monitor account health and intervene before churn.
use_cases.audience: Customer success teams at mid-market and enterprise SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Customer onboarding and renewals
use_cases.description: Teams manage the full customer journey, from onboarding through renewal, using branded customer portals.
use_cases.audience: CS and account management teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ClientSuccess?
paragraphs: ClientSuccess is a customer success platform built to reduce churn and grow customer revenue through health tracking, journey management, and an AI co-pilot called SmartCS.
Its SuccessScore system combines usage, adoption, feedback, and sentiment data into a single 360-degree view of account health.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ClientSuccess is for
paragraphs: ClientSuccess targets mid-market to enterprise B2B SaaS companies that want to scale customer success operations across the full customer lifecycle.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source help desk and shared inbox built with PHP/Laravel that you self-host.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: FreeScout is a free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) self-hosted help desk and shared inbox with unlimited agents, tickets, and mailboxes.

features.title: Unlimited agents and mailboxes
features.description: No limits on the number of agents, tickets, or mailboxes.

features.title: Mobile-friendly with native apps
features.description: Includes dedicated Android and iOS apps alongside a mobile-friendly web UI.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Supports 30+ languages for the interface.

features.title: Kanban boards and knowledge base
features.description: Optional modules add Kanban-style boards and a self-service knowledge base.

features.title: Channel integrations
features.description: Supports SMS via Twilio and integrations with Slack, WhatsApp, Facebook, and Telegram.

features.title: Security features
features.description: Supports S/MIME and PGP encryption, GDPR compliance, and SAML SSO.

pros: Core software is completely free and open source under AGPL-3.0
Unlimited agents, tickets, and mailboxes with no per-seat pricing
Native mobile apps for Android and iOS
Broad channel support including SMS, WhatsApp, and Telegram
cons: Requires self-hosting on PHP/MySQL infrastructure, needing technical setup
Advanced modules (Kanban, live chat, CRM, SMS) may require paid add-ons
Cloud-hosted options are only available via third-party providers, not officially from FreeScout

faqs.question: Is FreeScout really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core FreeScout software is free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host FreeScout?
faqs.answer: Yes, FreeScout is designed to be self-hosted on PHP/MySQL infrastructure, though third-party providers offer cloud hosting.

faqs.question: What is FreeScout built with?
faqs.answer: It's built with PHP using the Laravel framework.

faqs.question: Does FreeScout have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers dedicated Android and iOS apps in addition to a mobile-friendly web interface.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted shared inbox
use_cases.description: Support teams that want a Zendesk/Help Scout alternative without recurring per-agent fees self-host FreeScout.
use_cases.audience: Support teams wanting a self-hosted help desk

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious support
use_cases.description: Organizations with data-residency or privacy requirements run FreeScout on their own servers to keep ticket data in-house.
use_cases.audience: Privacy- and compliance-focused teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FreeScout?
paragraphs: FreeScout is a free, open-source help desk and shared inbox built with PHP and the Laravel framework, licensed under AGPL-3.0.
It positions itself as a self-hosted alternative to commercial help desks like Zendesk and Help Scout, with unlimited agents, tickets, and mailboxes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FreeScout is for
paragraphs: FreeScout suits support teams and organizations that want a self-hosted, privacy-friendly help desk without per-agent SaaS pricing, and that have the technical resources to run PHP/MySQL infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source (AGPL-3.0) helpdesk platform with AI features, available self-hosted or as German-hosted cloud SaaS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zammad is an open-source AGPL-3.0 helpdesk with AI ticket tools, self-hosted for free or cloud-hosted from €7/month.

features.title: Multi-channel ticketing
features.description: Centralizes email, chat, SMS, social media, and phone support in one platform.

features.title: AI ticket tools
features.description: Provides ticket summaries and writing assistance, billed per AI call on cloud plans.

features.title: Automation
features.description: Smart templates, bulk actions, and workflow automation for routine ticket handling.

features.title: Knowledge base
features.description: Multi-language knowledge base for both customers and agents.

features.title: Security
features.description: Two-factor authentication, S/MIME encryption, SSO, and PGP support.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: 30+ integrations including Microsoft Teams, Slack, and GitHub.

pros: Core software is open source under AGPL-3.0 and free to self-host
Cloud plans hosted on ISO27001-certified, GDPR-compliant German servers
Wide channel coverage including phone, WhatsApp, and Facebook on higher tiers
30-day free trial available on cloud plans
cons: AI features on cloud plans incur additional per-call fees (€0.03 per AI call)
Self-hosted support contracts start at €2,999/year, a significant cost beyond the free software itself
Lower cloud tiers cap the number of agents (5 on Starter, 35 on Professional)

faqs.question: Is Zammad free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Zammad software is open source under AGPL-3.0 and free to self-host; paid cloud hosting and support plans are also available.

faqs.question: How much does Zammad cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Cloud plans start at about €7/month (billed annually) for the Starter tier and go up to €25/month for the Plus tier with unlimited agents.

faqs.question: Does Zammad support AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, optional AI features like ticket summaries are available on Professional and higher cloud tiers, billed at €0.03 per AI call.

faqs.question: Where is Zammad's cloud hosted?
faqs.answer: On ISO27001-certified, GDPR-compliant servers in Germany.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted helpdesk
use_cases.description: Teams that want full control over their support infrastructure self-host Zammad for free.
use_cases.audience: Support teams with in-house infrastructure

use_cases.title: Managed cloud helpdesk
use_cases.description: Teams that prefer not to manage servers use Zammad's German-hosted cloud plans.
use_cases.audience: Support, IT, and internal operations teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zammad?
paragraphs: Zammad is an open-source helpdesk platform, licensed under AGPL-3.0, that centralizes support tickets across email, chat, SMS, social media, and phone.
It can be self-hosted for free or used as a managed cloud service hosted on ISO27001-certified servers in Germany, with optional AI features like ticket summaries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zammad is for
paragraphs: Zammad suits support, IT, and internal operations teams that want an open-source helpdesk they can either self-host for free or run as GDPR-compliant cloud SaaS.
